Package fr.maif

Class FeatureClientErrorStrategy<T extends FeatureClientErrorStrategy<T>>

  • java.lang.Object
    • fr.maif.FeatureClientErrorStrategy<T>
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    FeatureClientErrorStrategy.CallbackStrategy, FeatureClientErrorStrategy.DefaultValueStrategy, FeatureClientErrorStrategy.FailStrategy, FeatureClientErrorStrategy.NullValueStrategy
    public abstract class FeatureClientErrorStrategy<T extends FeatureClientErrorStrategy<T>>
extends Object
    Strategy to use when one feature activation status can't be fetched or computed locally. Izanami handle errors as follows :
    1. If feature can't be retrieved from server, the client will use last cached version of the feature (event if cache is disabled) to compute activation status. This computation is possible only for non script features.
    2. If the client is configured to not use last cached version, error strategy will be used
    Therefore, error strategy are used in 3 scenarios :
    • When feature can't be fetched from Izanami and has never been fetched before
    • When feature can't be fetched and the client is not allowed to use last feature status to compute it's activation
    • When feature can't be fetched and it's a script feature
    Error strategy can be specified at client level, query level and query feature levels. Priority is : query feature strategy over query strategy over client strategy

      • fallbackOnLastKnownStrategy

        public T fallbackOnLastKnownStrategy​(boolean shouldUseLastKnownStrategy)
        Parameters:
        shouldUseLastKnownStrategy - Indicate whether client is allowed to use last fetched feature state to compute activation status locally if feature retrieval goes wrong. Default is true.
        Returns:
        updated strategy

      • nullValueStrategy

        public static FeatureClientErrorStrategy.NullValueStrategy nullValueStrategy()

      • failStrategy

        public static FeatureClientErrorStrategy.FailStrategy failStrategy()
        Returns:
        an error strategy that either throws an IzanamiException (if query was a single feature query) or values feature activation status to null (if query was a multiple feature query).

      • defaultValueStrategy

        @Deprecated
public static FeatureClientErrorStrategy.DefaultValueStrategy defaultValueStrategy​(boolean defaultValue)
        Deprecated.
        Parameters:
        defaultValue - default value to use as activation status
        Returns:
        an error strategy that use provided value as activation status

        Parameters:
        callback - function that will be called to compute feature activation status
        Returns:
